summary: Tested admin console, Wingman stubs, and live-chat features end-to-end. Added /app/backend/tests/test_admin_wingman_chat.py with 32 tests covering admin login (success/wrong pw/me endpoint), auth guards on all 8 admin/Wingman GET endpoints (unauthed 401, authed 200), stats shape, booking PATCH flow (status-update+persist+invalid status 400 + unknown id 404), Wingman webhooks (unauthenticated POST + authenticated GET for leads/research/competitors), chat message end-to-end (Gemini 3 Flash reply, conversation continuity, admin conversation list + messages chronological), and regression (/, /bookings, /contact, /bookings/availability). All 32 tests PASS. Also ran a Playwright flow against the external preview URL verifying: (a) #ai-quote REMOVED from public site, (b) click-to-call button with href='tel:0490507878', (c) live-chat-toggle opens live-chat-panel, (d) navbar Book Now CTA (no 'Quote' link), (e) 'Live availability' badge in booking section, (f) /admin redirects to /admin/login without token, (g) wrong password shows 'Incorrect', (h) correct password '1302' stores token as pb_admin_token and redirects to /admin, (i) Overview tiles match /api/admin/stats values, (j) Wingman webhook URLs visible on dashboard. Found ONE real backend bug: brute-force lockout is not triggering behind the K8s ingress because request.client.host is the ingress pod IP which rotates per request — only 2 of 5 failed attempts landed on the same IP, so the 5-attempt threshold never fires.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout does NOT trigger in production/preview. request.client.host resolves to the ingress pod IP which rotates across requests. Verified: 5 wrong-password attempts from one external client produced 5×401 responses, and the 6th wrong attempt ALSO returned 401 (should be 429); db.login_attempts showed only 2 attempts on one IP because the rest were spread across other proxy IPs. Mitigation: read X-Forwarded-For (take the left-most IP) or X-Real-IP with fallback to request.client.host. Given the password is only 4 digits, this is HIGH priority.

minor.issue: Accepts valid statuses but does not guarantee atomic write under concurrency (inherited from iteration 2 note).
Webhooks are fully unauthenticated. Fine for MVP per requirements, but once Wingman is live consider an X-Wingman-Token header check.

integration_issues.issue: data-testid='live-chat-send' not found on the send button (the panel opens and input works, but automated send-click could not locate the submit control). Verify the send button exposes data-testid='live-chat-send' for stable automation. Backend chat endpoint itself works (pytest passes).

issues: An FAQ item still reads 'How accurate is the AI quote?' — AI Quote feature has been removed from the site, so this FAQ is now stale and should be updated or deleted.
Stat tiles lack data-testid='stat-<key>' attributes (found 0). Values render correctly, but adding testids would improve test coverage.
data-testids for Wingman nav buttons (nav-wingman-leads/research/competitors) were not found — they may be named differently. Visible labels work. Non-blocking.

action_items: FIX HIGH: In /app/backend/server.py admin_login, derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (left-most) with fallback to request.client.host. Otherwise 4-digit password '1302' is effectively brute-forceable from the internet.
Remove or rewrite FAQ item 'How accurate is the AI quote?' on public site — the AI Quote feature was removed.
Add data-testid='live-chat-send' to the chat submit button for stable E2E testing.
(Optional) Add data-testid='stat-<key>' to Overview tiles and data-testid='nav-wingman-*' to sidebar Wingman buttons for better testability.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 434: ip = request.client.host — unreliable behind ingress/proxy. Must use X-Forwarded-For to make brute-force protection effective.

server.py line 34: ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH = bcrypt.hashpw(...) is computed ONCE at module import using the env value. Correct, but note: if ADMIN_PASSWORD env changes, the server must be restarted (expected behavior; just calling it out).

server.py chat endpoint: every user message opens a new LlmChat session keyed by conversation_id — this means prior turns are NOT fed back to Gemini. The assistant has no memory of earlier messages in the same conversation beyond what the provider may cache by session_id. Consider fetching prior messages and including them in UserMessage or using chat_history.
server.py: Wingman POST endpoints accept a full WingmanLead including an 'id' field from the client. A client could overwrite/fake ids. Minor; consider reassigning id server-side.
server.py booking_availability: no check that the date is not far in the past (matches iter-2 observation).

success_rate.backend: 100% (32/32 pytest passes, plus 1 discovered runtime bug in brute-force lockout not caught by tests because all pytest requests share the same rotating proxy pool)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all key selectors and flows verified; minor testability gaps for live-chat-send + a few nav testids)

seed_data_creation: Booking records on 2031-01-05, 2031-01-06, 2031-02-02 (TEST_ prefixed); Wingman lead/research/competitor entries prefixed TEST_W*; chat conversations from 'Hi, how much for a split system clean?' test — all prefixed TEST_ where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite split into two files: test_purebreeze_api.py (public endpoints — health, LLM quote, contact, bookings, leads) and test_admin_wingman_chat.py (admin auth, Wingman, chat, patch flow, regressions). Admin password is 1302 stored in backend/.env as ADMIN_PASSWORD. Token in localStorage key pb_admin_token. Brute-force lockout test is intentionally NOT in pytest because (a) it would pollute the login_attempts collection and cascade-fail other tests, (b) it requires the real-client-IP fix to work at all. To manually retest lockout after fix: run `mongosh test_database --eval 'db.login_attempts.deleteMany({})'` then 5x POST /api/admin/login with wrong password from a single external client; 6th must return 429. Frontend URL: https://aircon-quote-now.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: Brute-force RCA: FastAPI's request.client.host returns the TCP peer address. In a K8s deployment behind an nginx/ingress controller with multiple proxy pods, the peer is the ingress pod, NOT the real client. The ingress rotates connections across pods, so 5 consecutive requests from the same real browser produce up to 5 different server-perceived ips. With count-per-ip logic, the 5-attempt threshold is almost never reached. Reproduction: `mongosh test_database --eval 'db.login_attempts.deleteMany({})'` then `for i in 0..4 curl POST /api/admin/login { password:'bad' }`; observe db.login_attempts has fewer than 5 total across 1+ IP records. Fix: `ip = (request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') or request.client.host).split(',')[0].strip()`. After fix, run the retest steps above.
